Smart Home Solutions for Seattle Living

Transform your Pacific Northwest home with intelligent technology tailored to our unique climate. From moisture-resistant automation to earthquake-ready security, we make homes work smarter.

SEE ALL SERVICES →

Who We Are

Built for Seattle's Homes

Founded in 2012, Horizon Smart Home began when our founder noticed how Seattle's damp climate wreaked havoc on traditional home systems. We pioneered moisture-resistant smart home installations that actually last through our rainy winters.

Our approach combines premium technology with deep local knowledge. We don't just install devices - we engineer systems that account for everything from Craftsman home layouts to moss growth patterns on rooftops.

As a proud member of the Seattle Smart Home Alliance, we're committed to making cutting-edge technology accessible across all our neighborhoods, from Capitol Hill's historic homes to Bellevue's modern estates.

12+ Years
800+ Projects
97% Satisfaction
GET IN TOUCH →

Our Difference

Why Choose Horizon Smart Home

Climate-Adaptive Tech

Our systems are specifically designed to handle Seattle's persistent moisture, with waterproof sensors and mildew-resistant materials.

Local Expertise

We understand the unique challenges of Seattle homes, from 1920s wiring to modern smart home integrations.

Earthquake Ready

All installations include seismic safeguards to protect your investment during tremors.

Neighborhood Specialists

Whether you're in a Ballard bungalow or a Bellevue mansion, we know your home's specific needs.

Ready for a Smarter Seattle Home?

Schedule your free smart home consultation today. Our local experts will assess your space and recommend the perfect climate-adaptive solutions.

Get a Free Estimate Call 555-sma-r90

Client Feedback

What Our Clients Say

“

Horizon transformed our 1920s Craftsman in Ballard without compromising its historic charm. They hid all the modern tech so well that guests don't realize our home is automated until we demonstrate it. The moisture sensors have been a game-changer - we used to battle mold every winter, but now the system automatically adjusts humidity levels. Worth every penny for Seattle homeowners.

Mikaela R. Whole Home Automation
“

After three packages were stolen from our Capitol Hill porch, we invested in Horizon's security system. The cameras can actually see through Seattle's rain, and the motion detection ignores neighborhood cats but alerted us to a would-be intruder at 2 AM. The police were impressed with the clarity of the footage even in pouring rain.

Daniel T. Smart Security Systems
“

Our modern Bellevue home had terrible hot/cold spots and condensation issues. Horizon's climate system not only fixed these problems but also reduced our energy bill by 25%. The predictive feature is amazing - it starts adjusting before weather changes hit. No more waking up to a freezing house when our infamous cold snaps arrive overnight.

Priya K. Climate Control Systems
“

The circadian lighting has made Seattle winters so much more bearable. Our system gradually brightens to simulate sunrise, which helps our whole family wake up more naturally during those dark December mornings. Horizon even matched the vintage switches in our Queen Anne home perfectly - you can't tell they're smart controls unless you know what to look for.

James L. Smart Lighting Solutions
“

As environmentally conscious Seattleites, we wanted to reduce our carbon footprint. Horizon's system automatically shifts our laundry and dishwasher to off-peak hours and gives us real-time usage data. We've cut our electricity use by 35% without changing our habits. The solar integration was seamless too - they handled all the paperwork with Seattle City Light.

Sophia M. Home Energy Management
“

Living in a waterfront Kirkland home means constant battles with moisture. Horizon's system not only automates our lighting and security but actively protects against mold and mildew. The earthquake protocols give us peace of mind too. Their team understood our unique lakeside challenges and designed solutions that actually work in our environment.

Ethan W. Whole Home Automation

Our Portfolio

Our Recent Work

Common Questions

Frequently Asked Questions

Will automation work in my 1930s Craftsman?
Absolutely. We specialize in retrofitting historic Seattle homes with invisible technology. Our solutions preserve original character while adding modern convenience, often using wireless systems that require minimal invasive installation.
How does the system handle power outages?
All our systems include battery backups rated for Seattle's storm seasons. Critical functions like security and leak detection remain operational for up to 72 hours without power, with automatic notifications if backup power runs low.
